title: Ledger record manifest
description: The serialized descriptive + access manifest for one archived item. A record NEVER carries a contributor identity: identity is encoded only as the opaque `identity_ref` token resolvable solely through the encrypted vault. This schema structurally forbids any top-level `contributor_name`, `identity`, or `name` property (no-outing rule).

record_id.description: Stable opaque identifier for the record.

title.description: Human-readable title of the archived item.

default_policy.description: Disclosure level applied where a field or payload does not state its own.

dublin_core.description: Collection-level Dublin Core description. Never an individual contributor's identity.

fields.description: Structured descriptive fields, each with its own disclosure policy.

payloads.description: Content-addressed files in the bag, each with its own disclosure policy.

content_warnings.description: Advisory content warnings shown ahead of disclosure.

identity_ref.description: Opaque token into the encrypted identity vault, or null. NEVER a name or contact.

dublinCore.description: DCMI Metadata Element Set (ISO 15836). Every element is repeatable, so each is an array of strings; empty elements are omitted.

unseal_at.description: ISO-8601 instant at which a sealed-until field opens, or null.

unseal_condition.description: Free-text condition for a sealed-conditional field, or null.

transcript.description: A flat, plain-text caption/transcript for an audio or video payload (accessibility, WCAG 1.2). Disclosed under this payload's own `policy`, never a policy of its own.

cues.description: RM6: the same transcript with real segment/timing structure, when it was ingested from a WebVTT or SRT caption file rather than typed as a single block of plain text. Empty when only the flat `transcript` was supplied. Disclosed under this payload's own `policy` — there is deliberately no per-cue policy (see the TranscriptCue docstring in ledger.models for the open per-cue-consent-granularity question).

transcriptCue.description: One timed segment of a caption/transcript track (a WebVTT cue or SRT block).

start.description: Normalized `HH:MM:SS.mmm` start time (WebVTT's own timestamp grammar), regardless of whether the cue was parsed from WebVTT or SRT.

end.description: Normalized `HH:MM:SS.mmm` end time; always later than `start`.

speaker.description: A speaker label from a WebVTT voice span (`<v Speaker Name>`), or null. SRT has no standardized speaker syntax, so an SRT-derived cue is always null here rather than a guessed value.
